ddls:
CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the application */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the application */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workloads" (
  "workload_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workload */,
  "workload_name" TEXT /* Name of the workload */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the workload */,
  "description_detail" TEXT /* Detailed description of the workload */,
  "config" TEXT /* Configuration settings for the workload */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workload_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy measurement */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application being measured */,
  "workload_id" INTEGER /* ID of the workload being applied */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system on which the measurement was taken */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the measurement was taken */,
  "fullboot_energy" REAL /* Energy consumption during full boot (in Joules) */,
  "fullboot_deviation" REAL /* Deviation in energy consumption during full boot (in Joules) */,
  "silentfullboot_energy" REAL /* Energy consumption during silent full boot (in Joules) */,
  "silentfullboot_deviation" REAL /* Deviation in energy consumption during silent full boot (in Joules) */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage during the measurement (in %) */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage during the measurement (in %) */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage during the measurement (in %) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes about the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_measurements_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_measurements_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_measurements_workload_id FOREIGN KEY ("workload_id") REFERENCES workloads ("workload_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, data analyst, researcher) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the energy measurement being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the data was accessed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent information (e.g., browser, device) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES energy_measurements ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "systems" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the system */,
  "system_type" TEXT /* Type of the system (e.g., server, workstation, mobile device) */,
  "cpu_model" TEXT /* Model of the CPU */,
  "ram_size" INTEGER /* Size of the RAM (in GB) */,
  "disk_size" INTEGER /* Size of the disk (in GB) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_configurations" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id", "config_name")
)
